verdicts.note: Evidence confirms the name 'Firecrawl' across all documentation pages.
Website firecrawl.dev is referenced throughout the evidence.
Evidence confirms Firecrawl is a web scraping/crawling API for data extraction.
Evidence confirms web scraping/crawling API, markdown/JSON output, self-serve signup, free tier, keyless access, and MCP server existence.

API key auth confirmed via Bearer token (Authorization: Bearer fc-YOUR-API-KEY). WorkOS ID-JAG mentioned in v2 docs for agent platforms. OAuth bearer token support mentioned in one_liner but not detailed in evidence—marking as partially supported.
Evidence confirms self-serve-free tier with 1,000 monthly credits, keyless mode with no account needed, and paid plans starting at $16/month ($19 regular, $16 annual).

REST API confirmed with endpoints for scrape, parse, batch/scrape, crawl, map, extract, search, deep-research/agent, browser sessions, and billing/usage. Python and Node SDKs mentioned. Medium breadth is appropriate.

The URL provided (https://docs.firecrawl.dev/mcp-server) is a documentation page for Firecrawl's MCP server, not a standalone MCP server repository. The keyless blog post confirms an MCP endpoint at https://mcp.firecrawl.dev/v2/mcp. The exists='yes' is correct, but the URL should point to the actual MCP endpoint or be left blank if only documentation exists.
Evidence confirms buildable-now with REST API, self-serve signup, free tier, keyless mode, and SDKs available.
No blockers identified; null is appropriate given buildable-now status.
All evidence URLs are valid and support the claims made in the record.
0.9 confidence is reasonable given comprehensive evidence for all major claims.

revised.name: Firecrawl
revised.one_liner: Firecrawl is a web scraping/crawling API that turns websites into LLM-ready markdown/JSON, with self-serve API key signup, a free tier, keyless rate-limited access, and an official MCP server.

auth_methods.notes: Bearer token format: Authorization: Bearer fc-YOUR-API-KEY, obtained from Firecrawl dashboard or set as FIRECRAWL_API_KEY environment variable
WorkOS ID-JAG identity assertions for agent platforms credential registration; MCP server supports keyless access (no auth) with IP-based rate limiting

revised.self_serve_notes: Free tier with 1,000 monthly credits available via signup (API key) or fully keyless mode (no account needed, IP-capped rate limits, basic scrape/search/interact only). Paid plans start at $16/month (annual) or $19/month for higher limits.

api_surface.notes: Endpoints for scrape, parse, batch/scrape, crawl, map, extract, search, deep-research/agent, browser sessions, and billing/usage. Base URL: https://api.firecrawl.dev. SDKs available for Python and Node.

evidence.supports: Bearer token auth via Authorization header, API key from dashboard, WorkOS ID-JAG alternative for agents, REST endpoints list (scrape, parse, crawl, map, search, agent, browser), base URL https://api.firecrawl.dev
Full REST API endpoint list including scrape, batch/scrape, crawl, map, extract, search, deep-research, billing; API key signup process via firecrawl.dev dashboard
Free tier with 1,000 monthly credits, no credit card required, paid plans starting at $19/month or $16/month billed annually
Keyless mode allows CLI/MCP/SDK usage without API key or account, IP-based rate limiting, 1,000 free credits per month; MCP endpoint at https://mcp.firecrawl.dev/v2/mcp
Python SDK usage with API key or FIRECRAWL_API_KEY env var; keyless mode available for scrape, search, and interact with IP-based rate limits
